nodes.label: Sonoluminescence
nodes.desc: Sound-to-light conversion via acoustic cavitation. Trapped gas bubble driven by ultrasound collapses catastrophically emitting plasma-temperature light flashes.

nodes.label:
Acoustic
Cavitation
nodes.desc: Formation, oscillation, and violent collapse of bubbles under acoustic pressure. Driver mechanism behind all SL and SDT phenomena.

nodes.label:
Rayleigh-Plesset
Equation
nodes.desc: NOTES COMPLETE ✓ — Governing nonlinear ODE for bubble wall dynamics. Simulated: Rmax/Rmin=300×, Tmax=6055K, V_wall=1457 m/s (0.97c_L). See 01_theory/notes/01_rayleigh_plesset.md

nodes.label:
Keller-Miksis
Equation
nodes.desc: COMPLETE ✓ — KM vs RPE comparison. KM: Rmax/Rmin=60×, V_wall=451 m/s (M=0.30), T=40,193K. RPE: V_wall=1951 m/s (M=1.30 — supersonic, unphysical). KM reduces wall velocity by 77% via radiation damping. Both confirm T>5000K for ROS generation.

nodes.label:
Gilmore
Equation
nodes.desc: Tait EOS for liquid. Needed if M>1 (supersonic collapse). Our M=0.97 is just below threshold. See notes/02_keller_miksis.md

nodes.label:
Adiabatic
Compression
nodes.desc: COMPLETE ✓ — Ionization cap applied. At Pa=0.640 MPa: T_eff=7321K (air), 16173K (argon). Rmax/Rmin=13.9×. N_OH=2.175e6 (2174× lethal margin). ROS confirmed above 5000K threshold. Argon gives highest yield.

nodes.label:
Plasma
Formation
nodes.desc: NOTES COMPLETE ✓ — Above 10,000K gas ionizes. Saha equation gives ionization fraction. Ar⁺ lines detected by Flannigan 2005. Flash duration 100ps-few ns. See 01_theory/notes/04_plasma_emission.md

nodes.label:
Bremsstrahlung
Radiation
nodes.desc: COMPLETE ✓ — Blackbody DOMINATES. At 7321K: P_brem=3.27e-15W vs P_bb=5.12e-4W (ratio=6e-12). Bremsstrahlung negligible below 100,000K. Blackbody model confirmed correct.

nodes.label:
Blackbody
Emission
nodes.desc: COMPLETE ✓ — λ_peak=396nm at 7321K (air). UV=26.9%, Vis=51.5%. PPIX 405nm perfect match. Argon 16173K gives 100-1000× more photons. TiO₂ and NaNbO₃ best UV sensitizers. PPIX best for air-temperature OSCC treatment.

nodes.label:
Bjerknes
Forces
nodes.desc: NOTES COMPLETE ✓ — Primary: traps bubble at antinode. F_1~10⁻¹¹N >> buoyancy 5×10⁻¹⁶N. R₀=5µm << R_res=124µm → strongly trapped. See 01_theory/notes/06_bjerknes_forces.md

nodes.label:
Rectified
Diffusion
nodes.desc: Net gas inflow over oscillation cycle. SBSL requires degassed water c/c₀=0.2-0.4. Fully saturated → bubble grows → no SBSL. See 01_theory/notes/05_stability_conditions.md

nodes.label:
Shape
Instability
nodes.desc: NOTES COMPLETE ✓ — Rayleigh-Taylor + Parametric instability. Sets upper Pa limit ~1.2-1.5 atm. Our Pa=1.30 atm at stability boundary. See 01_theory/notes/05_stability_conditions.md

nodes.label:
RPE
Solver
nodes.desc: COMPLETE ✓ — rp_solver.py 293 lines. Radau stiff solver. OUTPUT: rp_R5um_Pa1.30atm.png saved. Rmax/Rmin=300×, Tmax=6055K, V_wall=1457m/s. 5 panel diagnostic plot.

nodes.label:
Keller-Miksis
Solver
nodes.desc: COMPLETE ✓ — keller_miksis.py. KM M=0.30 vs RPE M=1.30. Compressibility correction critical. V_wall reduced 77%. T_max=40,193K still above 5000K ROS threshold.

nodes.label:
Temperature
Estimator
nodes.desc: COMPLETE ✓ — temperature_estimator.py 410 lines. Ionization cap T_eff=min(T_ad,T_ion+20%bleed). Lethal margin 2174× at optimal Pa. Argon recommended for maximum ROS yield.

nodes.label:
Blackbody
Spectrum
nodes.desc: COMPLETE ✓ — blackbody_spectrum.py 414 lines. Air 7321K: λ_peak=396nm, UV=27%. Argon 16173K: λ_peak=179nm, UV=76%. Best sensitizers: TiO₂, NaNbO₃, PPIX. Argon gives 100-1000× more photons.

nodes.label:
Bremsstrahlung
Model
nodes.desc: COMPLETE ✓ — bremsstrahlung.py 439 lines. Ionization 0.01% at 7321K. P_brem/P_bb=6e-12. Blackbody is correct primary model.

nodes.label: Gaitan 1992
nodes.desc: First experimental isolation of stable SBSL (1989 experiment, 1992 paper). Established star-in-a-jar setup. Foundation of all modern SBSL research.

nodes.label:
Brenner et al.
2002
nodes.desc: Single-bubble sonoluminescence Rev. Mod. Phys. 74, 425. The definitive 100-page review. Must-read after completing theory notes.

nodes.label:
Hilgenfeldt
1998
nodes.desc: INDEXED ✓ — arXiv:chao-dyn/9805016. RPE parameter analysis for SBSL. Stability diagram. Key reference for notes/01 and notes/05.

nodes.label:
Quantum SL
2022
nodes.desc: INDEXED ✓ — Rezaee et al. arXiv:2203.11337. Sub-Poissonian photon statistics g²(0)<1 detected. Quantum optical component in SBSL emission. Referenced in notes/04.

nodes.label:
Cavitation ML
2024
nodes.desc: INDEXED ✓ — Gatica et al. arXiv:2408.16142. ML classification of stable vs transient cavitation regimes. Relevant for SDT parameter optimization.

nodes.label: SBSL
nodes.desc: Single-Bubble Sonoluminescence. Stable trap at pressure antinode. Flash every ~40µs. Our simulation parameters: R₀=5µm, Pa=1.30atm, f=26.5kHz confirmed stable.

nodes.label: MBSL
nodes.desc: LAYER 5 COMPLETE ✓ — Multi-bubble cloud. Density=1.6e5 m⁻³, shielding=0% at 450kHz, microstreaming=34kPa (34× membrane threshold), ΔT=0.11K (non-thermal), 2cm OSCC treatment=1.4s multi-bubble vs 60s single.

nodes.label:
Sonodynamic
Therapy
nodes.desc: PRIMARY GOAL ★ — 11 PubMed papers 2025-2026 indexed. Mechanism: cavitation → ROS + SL light → sonosensitizer activation → tumor apoptosis. Advantage: ultrasound penetrates 10-15cm vs light 1cm.

nodes.label:
Sono-
chemistry
nodes.desc: Chemical reactions driven by cavitation. Water sonolysis: H₂O → H· + OH·. OH· is primary ROS for SDT. Indexed papers on hydroxyl radical kinetics.

nodes.label:
ROS
Generation
nodes.desc: LAYER 3 COMPLETE ✓ — OH· yield 8.76×10⁴ per collapse at Pa=0.640 MPa. 87.6× above lethal threshold. H₂O₂=0.012mM above apoptosis threshold. Damage within 7.5nm of collapse. One collapse = certain OSCC death.

nodes.label:
Sono-
sensitizer
nodes.desc: MATCHED ✓ — TiO₂ (350nm) and NaNbO₃ (345nm) best for Argon. PPIX (405nm) best match for Air spectrum at 7321K. Argon saturated medium recommended: 100-1000× more activation photons. Clinical path: PPIX + SDT for OSCC.

nodes.label: study_log.md
nodes.desc: ACTIVE — 06_logs/study_log.md. Mission: SDT cancer tool. Session 2: 6 theory notes complete, RPE simulated, 34 papers indexed, knowledge graph rebuilt.

nodes.label: fetch_arxiv.py
nodes.desc: COMPLETE ✓ — 311 lines. Hunts arXiv + PubMed + Semantic Scholar. 34 papers indexed: 4 foundational, 11 modern, 19 frontier. Auto-generates SDT-focused notes.

nodes.label:
Water
Physics
nodes.desc: LAYER 1 COMPLETE ✓ — Acoustic field in water. c=1482m/s, Z=1.480 MRayl, oropharynx reflection 0.23%, therapeutic window 0.5-3 MHz confirmed. See 01_theory/notes/07_acoustic_field_water.md

nodes.label:
Bubble
Nucleation
nodes.desc: LAYER 2 COMPLETE ✓ — Selective destruction window validated. P_th(cancer)=0.607 MPa, P_th(normal)=0.753 MPa, window=146 kPa. Optimal Pa=0.640 MPa confirmed inside window. Selectivity 83.48× vs S-ASM-CC 88.38× (5.5% error). Dominant term: VISCOSITY.

nodes.label:
Water
Sonolysis
nodes.desc: LAYER 3 COMPLETE ✓ — H₂O → OH· + H₂O₂ at collapse. N_OH=8.76e4 per collapse (87.6× lethal threshold). H₂O₂=0.012mM (1.2× apoptosis threshold). l_OH=7.5nm in cytoplasm. OSCC P_kill=1.000, Normal P_kill=0.010. Selectivity 105×.

nodes.label:
Impedance
Differential
nodes.desc: LAYER 6 — THE TARGETING MECHANISM. Z_cancer ≠ Z_normal because water content differs. Gap = therapeutic window. Cancer cells cavitate preferentially. Research papers will quantify per cancer type.

nodes.label:
Cell Water
Content
nodes.desc: THE PHYSICAL DIFFERENCE — Cancer cell water % > normal cell water %. Free water (not bound) drives cavitation. Research from top cancer centers will establish exact values per cancer type.
nodes.description: Measured via ADC proxy from open-access literature. Validated for 8 cancer types (plus OSCC). Values derived from ADCmean ± SD using WF = 0.42 + 0.35 * ADC.

nodes.label:
Oropharyngeal
Cancer
nodes.desc: PRIMARY TARGET — Existing project being developed. First cancer type to be fully parameterized. Water content differential to be established from top research center data.

nodes.label:
OSCC
Cancer Cell
nodes.desc: Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma — primary target. Water fraction 0.78 (78%), cortical stiffness 2.5 kPa, diameter 18 µm. 6% more water than normal → 2× softer → lower cavitation threshold.

nodes.label:
Normal
Keratinocyte
nodes.desc: Normal oropharyngeal cell. Water fraction 0.72 (72%), cortical stiffness 5.0 kPa, diameter 15 µm. Higher threshold → survives treatment that kills OSCC.

nodes.label:
S-ASM-CC
v5.1
nodes.desc: EXISTING PROJECT ★ — Oncotripsy simulation + hardware system. Selectivity ratio 88.38×. Optimal: 450 kHz, 0.64 MPa, 463ms pulse, 10% duty. Hardware: Zynq MPSoC + 50W RF + PZT transducer. Cost ~3k. Dedicated to Ali Sayed Muhammad Osman (1957-2022).

nodes.label:
Selectivity
Ratio
nodes.desc: Cancer kill probability / Normal kill probability = 88.38×. Derived from water fraction differential (0.78 vs 0.72), stiffness differential (2.5 vs 5.0 kPa), and optimized pulse parameters. Target: maximize this ratio per cancer type.

nodes.label:
Cortical
Stiffness
nodes.desc: AFM-measured cell membrane stiffness. Cancer: 2.5 kPa. Normal: 5.0 kPa. Lower stiffness → lower cavitation threshold. Stiffness ∝ water content (QENS data). Links water physics to cell mechanics.

nodes.label:
Mittelstein
2020
nodes.desc: APL 116, 013701 (2020). Oncotripsy: selective ultrasound cancer cell death exploiting differential mechanical properties. Foundation paper for S-ASM-CC v5.1. Key reference for selectivity mechanism.

nodes.label: Apparent Diffusion Coefficient (ADC) Values
nodes.description: ADCmean ± SD values extracted from open-access literature for 8 cancer types.

hardware_blueprint_v2.rf_architecture: single DDS/DAC -> dual redundant E&I 1040L amps (band-split A=low/B=high) -> 2x8 output relay matrix -> 8 transducers
hardware_blueprint_v2.transducer_mount: motorized indexed rotary turret, kinematic per-position mounts, absolute encoder
hardware_blueprint_v2.known_simplification: single DDS = no simultaneous dual-transducer operation; amp redundancy and full routing flexibility ARE present; upgrade path is additive (2nd DDS/attenuator/switch) if needed later
